frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: recent stimuli bias subsequent perceptual decisions in feature space

frameworks.name: feature-based attention
frameworks.one_line: attentional role of targets vs distractors shapes direction of perceptual bias

core_question: Do visual search targets and distractors induce opposing serial dependencies on the perceived orientation of a neutral subsequent test line?
key_assumptions: perception at any moment is shaped by recent temporal context
to-be-ignored items can bias subsequent perceptual decisions in parallel with attended items
biases depend on proximity in feature space between prior and current stimuli
manipulations: attentional role of prior item (target vs distractor in visual search)
feature-space distance between prior items and the neutral test line orientation
key_findings: targets induced a positive (attractive) bias on a neutral test line when orientations were similar
distractors produced an attractive bias on similar test lines
distractors produced a repulsive bias when test orientation was far from the average distractor orientation in feature space
biases on a task-irrelevant neutral probe generalize beyond search-target judgments
interpretations: both attentional role and feature-space proximity jointly determine the direction of serial biases
search targets and distractors leave opposing perceptual traces that act in parallel
